canonical_question: How do I implement feature flags?
aliases: how to do progressive rollout with feature flags
feature flag types release experiment ops permission
feature flag cleanup and technical debt
percentage rollout canary release feature flags

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Martin Fowler's feature toggles taxonomy (2017) remains the industry standard; OpenFeature spec v0.7 (2024) standardizes SDK interfaces

constraints: Never expose flag evaluation logic to the client in security-sensitive contexts -- server-side evaluation prevents users from toggling flags via browser devtools
Always provide a default fallback value when evaluating a flag -- if the flag service is unreachable, the application must not crash or expose unfinished features
Never use feature flags to gate security controls (authentication, authorization, encryption) -- a misconfigured flag could disable security entirely
Remove feature flags within 30 days of full rollout -- stale flags accumulate as technical debt and create untested code paths
Test both flag-on and flag-off paths in CI -- untested branches become landmines in production

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need A/B testing with statistical significance analysis
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: A/B testing framework (Optimizely, Google Optimize, Statsig)
Environment variables or configuration management (dotenv, Vault, AWS SSM)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need role-based access control for application features

alternative_to.label: Role-Based Access Control (for permanent feature gating)

often_confused_with.label: RBAC -- permanent permission gating vs temporary feature rollout

inputs_needed.question: Will you use a managed service (LaunchDarkly, Unleash, Flagsmith) or build a custom implementation?
What is your primary backend language?
What type of feature flag do you need?
